title: Tech workers urge DOD, Congress to withdraw Anthropic label as a supply chain risk
description: Tech workers have signed an open letter urging the Department of War to withdraw its designation of Anthropic as a "supply chain risk" and instead to settle the matter quietly.

title: X adds ‘Paid Partnership’ labels so creators can ditch the hashtags
description: The new labels comply with regulations and allow creators to be more transparent with their followers.

title: Hacktivists claim to have hacked Homeland Security to release ICE contract data
description: A hacking group called Department of Peace said they hacked a specific office within Homeland Security to protest ICE’s mass deportation campaign, and the companies aiding it.

title: A married founder duo’s company, 14.ai, is replacing customer support teams at startups
description: 14.ai also launched a consumer brand to understand how much AI can handle customer support tasks.

title: Apple speeds up the iPad Air with an M4 upgrade, starting at $599
description: Apple refreshes the iPad Air with the M4 chip and more memory to make it better for AI use cases.

title: Apple bakes in AI smarts into its new $599 iPhone 17e
description: The base model comes with 256 GB of storage, which Apple says is twice the entry storage from the previous generation at the same starting price.

title: Hackers and internet outages hit Iran amid US air strikes
description: Users of a popular Iranian prayer app were flooded with phone notifications as U.S. air strikes hit Iran's biggest cities, killing the country's leader.

title: MyFitnessPal has acquired Cal AI, the viral calorie app built by teens
description: After wooing Cal AI for months, MyFitnessPal has successfully acquired its up-and-coming rival, Cal AI, the app built by teens soaring on the apps stores.

title: Parade’s Cami Tellez announces new creator economy marketing platform, $4M in funding
description: Cami Tellez announces launch of new company, $4 million raise.

title: Anthropic’s Claude reports widespread outage
description: Anthropic's AI chatbot Claude experienced widespread service disruptions on Monday morning, with thousands of users reporting issues accessing the bot.

title: Google looks to tackle longstanding RCS spam in India — but not alone
description: Google is integrating carrier-level filtering into RCS in India through a partnership with Airtel to strengthen protections against spam.

title: Investors spill what they aren’t looking for anymore in AI SaaS companies
description: TechCrunch spoke with VCs to learn what investors aren't looking for in AI SaaS startups anymore.

title: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
description: By CEO Sam Altman’s own admission, OpenAI’s deal with the Department of Defense was “definitely rushed,” and “the optics don’t look good.”

title: Honor says its ‘Robot phone’ with moving camera can dance to music
description: <![CDATA[Honor first teased its “Robot phone” with a movable camera arm earlier this year. Ahead of the Mobile World Congress (MWC) in Barcelona, the Chinese company provided more details about the device, including how the robot can respond to different situations without commands. The

title: Anthropic’s Claude rises to No. 1 in the App Store following Pentagon dispute
description: Anthropic’s chatbot Claude seems to have benefited from the attention around the company’s fraught negotiations with the Pentagon.

title: The trap Anthropic built for itself
description: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind and others have long promised to govern themselves responsibly. Now, in the absence of rules, there's not a lot to protect them.

title: Supreme Court won’t hear AI-generated art copyright case
Nvidia’s spending $4 billion on photonics to stay ahead of the curve in AI
